inputs.label: Total Cost of Batch
inputs.businessContext: Total cost incurred to produce the entire batch, including raw materials, labor, and overhead. Based on standard cost accounting principles (e.g., GAAP).

inputs.label: Batch Size (Number of Portions)
inputs.businessContext: Total number of portions produced in the batch. Used to calculate cost per portion. Standard in production planning.

inputs.label: Waste Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of batch lost due to waste (e.g., trimming, spoilage). Based on lean manufacturing principles (e.g., muda reduction).

inputs.label: Labor Cost per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Hourly labor cost including wages and benefits. Used to allocate labor cost per portion. Standard in cost accounting.

inputs.label: Labor Hours per Batch
inputs.businessContext: Total labor hours required to produce the batch. Used to compute labor cost per portion.

inputs.label: Overhead Rate
inputs.businessContext: Overhead cost as a percentage of direct costs (materials + labor). Based on standard cost allocation methods (e.g., activity-based costing).

inputs.label: Desired Profit Margin
inputs.businessContext: Target profit margin on cost. Used to calculate selling price per portion. Common in pricing strategy.

thresholds.wastePercentage > 10: High waste percentage detected; consider process improvement.
thresholds.overheadRate > 50: Overhead rate exceeds 50%; review overhead allocation.

suggestedActions: Reduce waste through better portion control or process optimization.
Review overhead allocation and reduce non-value-added activities.
Consider increasing batch size to spread fixed costs.
